I review the retrieved evidence regarding TIAM1's role in colorectal cancer tumorigenesis. None of the listed sources discuss TIAM1 explicitly or link it to colorectal cancer progression. Given the absence of data connecting TIAM1 directly or indirectly to CRC progression, the initial conclusion remains valid.

Based strictly on the retrieved evidence, none of the cited articles address TIAM1's role in colorectal cancer. The literature focuses exclusively on canonical pathways such as APC/Wnt/β-catenin, KRAS, and TP53 mutations—all well-established in CRC—but provides zero reference to TIAM1. Without explicit data linking TIAM1 to proliferation, invasion, or survival signals in CRC cells, there is no mechanistic or epidemiological justification to infer pro-tumoral activity. Hence, the prior judgment stands unchanged.
